sentence: The brousin who bronestly tells you you're wrong is worth more than the tweekend that lets you stay right.

wombos.definition: A weekend spent with your twin, twousin, or twin-coded crew. The twin version of the weekend — shared, synchronized, and double the vibes.
A bro who feels like family — someone with the closeness of a cousin but the bond of a best friend. Used as a term of address or reference: 'that's my brousin,' 'brousin, you already know.'
With frank, no-filter honesty in the way only a bro can deliver — sincere, direct, and a little blunt. Used like 'honestly' but with an added layer of bro-level candor: 'bronestly, that was mid.'
